title: Explicit handoff protocol for agent-to-agent control transfer
source: Search description: OpenAI Swarm / Agents SDK 'handoff' pattern + Anthropic 'Building Effective Agents' orchestrator-worker section (web fetch blocked this run; pattern is established)
description: Currently delegate.py spawns isolated `claude -p` subprocesses with no first-class way for one Morty to hand a task back to Rick or sideways to a specialist mid-run. Swarm-style handoffs make routing data-driven instead of hardcoded, so a worker that discovers it needs a different skill can transfer control with accumulated context rather than failing or guessing.
proposed_changes: Add a `Handoff` dataclass/schema in schemas.py (fields: target_role, reason, context_summary, ticket_id). In delegate.py, parse a structured `<handoff>` JSON block from a worker's final output; when present, re-delegate to the named role with context_summary injected into the new prompt instead of returning the raw result. In team.py, route handoffs through send_team_message so the transfer is visible on the roro event stream.
